title: CORS Misconfiguration Tester: Cross-Origin Audit Utility
description: A scanning script that systematically tests CORS endpoint configurations to identify null origins, wildcard reflections, and credentials exposure risks.
markdown:
### 📋 Project Overview

The **CORS Misconfiguration Tester** is an automated auditor designed to check web API endpoints for insecure Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) configurations. It allows developers to verify if their servers are reflecting origins unsafely, exposing sensitive resources to cross-site scripting (XSS) actions.

---

### 🛡️ Key Auditing Features

- **Origin Reflection Check**: Sends custom `Origin` headers to identify if the backend dynamically mirrors them inside the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header.
- **Null Origin Auditor**: Tests if the backend accepts `Origin: null`, which can allow local sandboxed files or iframe scripts to steal user sessions.
- **Credential Sharing Check**: Identifies if `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true` is configured alongside dynamic origin reflections, creating session hijacking paths.
- **Trusted Subdomain Bypass**: Attempts subdomain validation checks (e.g. `trusteddomain.com.attacker.com` or `attacker-trusteddomain.com`) to evaluate validation regex weaknesses.

---

### 🧠 Core Security Concepts

Cross-Origin Resource Sharing is a browser mechanism that enables cross-origin resource access. If a server reflection trusts any requesting origin and exposes session credentials (cookies, headers), any website visited by a user can query the vulnerable API to retrieve sensitive account data on behalf of that user.

---

### 🔗 Project Source & Reference

The codebase, installation steps, and CLI usage arguments are available on the official GitHub repository:
👉 [GitHub Repository: cors-tester](https://github.com/amooryx/cors-tester)
